'''月寒神社'''（つきさむじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]][[月寒]]西3条4丁目1－56にある[[神社]]。創祀は明治17年（1884年）である。[[福住厳島神社]]とは同根である。
== 由緒 == 明治17年（[[1884年]]）に、広島県出身の移住者が[[厳島神社]]の御分霊と稲荷神の2柱を現在の[[福住]]地区に祀った。明治33年（[[1900年]]）[[9月7日]]には北海道庁指令2307号を以って、無格社「西山神社」と公称し、創立許可を得た。明治36年（[[1903年]]）[[10月28日]]に月寒神社と改称し、月寒村17番地への移転を出願し、翌37年（[[1904年]]）5月24日には移転許可を受ける。これにより、現在の福住厳島神社と分離することになる。大正7年（[[1918年]]）[[2月16日]]には月寒498番地（現在地）への移転許可を出願し、同年[[3月4日]]に移転許可を受ける。同年[[11月2日]]には村社に昇格し、幣帛供進神社に指定される。昭和46年（[[1971年]]）[[12月2日]]には社殿・参集所・社務所御造営が竣功した。
== 祭神 == *[[倉稲魂命]]（うがのみたまのみこと） *[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]]（おおやまつみのみこと） *[[市杵島姫命]]（いちきしまひめのみこと） *宇摩志麻遲命（うましまぢのみこと）
== 交通 == *[[札幌市営地下鉄東豊線]][[月寒中央駅]]から徒歩15分程度 *[[北海道中央バス]]月寒中央通4丁目停留所から徒歩10分程度 {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:北海道の神社|つきさむ]]

'''月寒神社'''（つきさむじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]][[月寒]]西3条4丁目1－56にある[[神社]]。創祀は明治17年（1884年）である。[[福住厳島神社]]とは同根である。
== 由緒 == 明治17年（[[1884年]]）に、広島県出身の移住者が[[厳島神社]]の御分霊と稲荷神の2柱を現在の[[福住]]地区に祀った。明治33年（[[1900年]]）[[9月7日]]には北海道庁指令2307号を以って、無格社「西山神社」と公称し、創立許可を得た。明治36年（[[1903年]]）[[10月28日]]に月寒神社と改称し、月寒村17番地への移転を出願し、翌37年（[[1904年]]）5月24日には移転許可を受ける。これにより、現在の福住厳島神社と分離することになる。大正7年（[[1918年]]）[[2月16日]]には月寒498番地（現在地）への移転許可を出願し、同年[[3月4日]]に移転許可を受ける。同年[[11月2日]]には村社に昇格し、幣帛供進神社に指定される。昭和46年（[[1971年]]）[[12月2日]]には社殿・参集所・社務所御造営が竣功した。
== 祭神 == *[[倉稲魂命]]（うがのみたまのみこと） *[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]]（おおやまつみのみこと） *[[市杵島姫命]]（いちきしまひめのみこと） *宇摩志麻遲命（うましまぢのみこと）
== 交通 == *[[札幌市営地下鉄東豊線]][[月寒中央駅]]から徒歩15分程度 *[[北海道中央バス]]月寒中央通4丁目停留所から徒歩10分程度 {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:北海道の神社|つきさむしんしや]] [[Category:村社|つきさむしんしや]]

市杵島姫命
宇摩志麻遲命| 社格=村社| 創建=明治17年（1884年）}} '''月寒神社'''（つきさむじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]][[月寒]]にある[[神社]]。[[福住厳島神社]]とは同根である。 == 由緒 == [[1884年]]（[[明治]]17年）に、[[広島県]]出身の移住者が[[厳島神社]]の御分霊と稲荷神の2柱を現在の[[福住 (札幌市)|福住]]地区に祀った。[[1900年]]（明治33年）[[9月7日]]には[[北海道]]庁指令2307号を以って、無格社「'''西山神社'''」と公称し、創立許可を得た。[[1903年]]（明治36年）[[10月28日]]に「'''月寒神社'''」と改称し、月寒村17番地への移転を出願し、翌[[1904年]]（明治37年）[[5月24日]]には移転許可を受ける。これにより、現在の[[福住厳島神社]]と分離することになる。 [[1918年]]（[[大正]]7年）[[2月16日]]には[[月寒]]498番地（現在地）への移転許可を出願し、同年[[3月4日]]に移転許可を受ける。同年[[11月2日]]には村社に昇格し、幣帛供進神社に指定される。[[1971年]]（[[昭和]]46年）[[12月2日]]には社殿・参集所・社務所御造営が竣功した。 なお、本来の例大祭は9月8日であるが、隣接して小学校が所在することなどから町内会等との申し合わせもあり、現在は毎年9月の第2日曜日に執り行われている。今後はこのような形態が増えると思われるが、祭日にしたがって移動する露天商からは出店しにくいとの意見も多いようである。 == 祭神 == *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（うがのみたまのみこと） *[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]]（おおやまつみのみこと） *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（いちきしまひめのみこと） *[[ウマシマヂ|宇摩志麻遲命]]（うましまぢのみこと） == 交通手段 == *[[札幌市営地下鉄東豊線]][[月寒中央駅]]から徒歩15分程度 *[[北海道中央バス]]月寒中央通4丁目[[バス停留所|停留所]]から徒歩10分程度 ==関連項目== *[[福住厳島神社]] [[Category:北海道の神社|つきさむしんしや]] [[Category:村社|つきさむしんしや]] [[Category:豊平区|つきさむしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: 露天商の都合などは、別に神社の由緒とは関係なし。

秋祭（11月23日） }} '''月寒神社'''（つきさむじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]][[月寒|月寒西]]にある[[神社]]。[[福住厳島神社]]とは同根である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。 == 由緒 == [[1884年]]（[[明治]]17年）に、[[広島県]]出身の移住者が[[厳島神社]]の御分霊と稲荷神の2柱を現在の[[福住 (札幌市)|福住]]地区に祀った。[[1900年]]（明治33年）[[9月7日]]には[[北海道]]庁指令2307号を以って、無格社「'''西山神社'''」と公称し、創立許可を得た。[[1903年]]（明治36年）[[10月28日]]に「'''月寒神社'''」と改称し、月寒村17番地への移転を出願し、翌[[1904年]]（明治37年）[[5月24日]]には移転許可を受ける。これにより、現在の[[福住厳島神社]]と分離することになる。 [[1918年]]（[[大正]]7年）[[2月16日]]には[[月寒]]498番地（現在地）への移転許可を出願し、同年[[3月4日]]に移転許可を受ける。同年[[11月2日]]には村社に昇格し、幣帛供進神社に指定される。[[1971年]]（[[昭和]]46年）[[12月2日]]には社殿・参集所・社務所御造営が竣功した。 なお、本来の例大祭は9月8日であるが、隣接して[[札幌市立月寒小学校|小学校]]が所在することなどから町内会等との申し合わせもあり、現在は毎年9月の第2日曜日に執り行われている。 == 祭神 == *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（うがのみたまのみこと） *[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]]（おおやまつみのみこと） *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（いちきしまひめのみこと） *[[ウマシマジ|宇摩志麻遲命]]（うましまぢのみこと） == 交通手段 == *[[札幌市営地下鉄東豊線]][[月寒中央駅]]から徒歩10分程度 *[[北海道中央バス]]月寒中央通4丁目[[バス停留所|停留所]]から徒歩5分程度 == 出典 == {{reflist}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきさむしんしや}} [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧村社]] [[Category:豊平区の歴史]] [[Category:豊平区の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称=月寒神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Tsukisamu-jinja main shrine.jpg|250px|月寒神社]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]]月寒西3条4丁目1-56 | 緯度度 = 43 | 緯度分 = 1 | 緯度秒 = 55 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 141 |経度分 = 23 | 経度秒 = 20.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Sapporo2 |祭神=[[倉稲魂命]]
[[大山祇命]]
[[市杵島姫命]]
宇摩志麻遲命{{Cite web |url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01022.html |title=月寒神社　 |publisher =北海道神社庁 公式ホームページ|accessdate =2018-06-06}} |社格=旧[[村社]] |創建=[[明治]]17年（[[1884年]]） |例祭=9月8日 |神事= }} '''月寒神社'''（つきさむじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]][[月寒|月寒西]]にある[[神社]]。[[福住厳島神社]]とは同根である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 由緒 == [[1884年]]（[[明治]]17年）に、[[広島県]]出身の移住者が[[厳島神社]]の御分霊と稲荷神の2柱を現在の[[福住 (札幌市)|福住]]地区に祀った。[[1900年]]（明治33年）[[9月7日]]には[[北海道]]庁指令2307号を以って、無格社「'''西山神社'''」と公称し、創立許可を得た。[[1903年]]（明治36年）[[10月28日]]に「'''月寒神社'''」と改称し、月寒村17番地への移転を出願し、翌[[1904年]]（明治37年）[[5月24日]]には移転許可を受ける。これにより、現在の[[福住厳島神社]]と分離することになる。 [[1918年]]（[[大正]]7年）[[2月16日]]には[[月寒]]498番地（現在地）への移転許可を出願し、同年[[3月4日]]に移転許可を受ける。同年[[11月2日]]には村社に昇格し、幣帛供進神社に指定される。[[1971年]]（[[昭和]]46年）[[12月2日]]には社殿・参集所・社務所御造営が竣功した。 なお、本来の例大祭は9月8日であるが、隣接して[[札幌市立月寒小学校|小学校]]が所在することなどから町内会等との申し合わせもあり、現在は毎年9月の第2日曜日に執り行われている。また[[都市公園]]である[[月寒公園]]とも隣接する。 == 祭神 == *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（うがのみたまのみこと） *[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]]（おおやまつみのみこと） *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（いちきしまひめのみこと） *[[ウマシマジ|宇摩志麻遲命]]（うましまぢのみこと） == 交通手段 == *[[札幌市営地下鉄東豊線]][[月寒中央駅]]から徒歩10分程度 *札幌市営地下鉄東豊線[[美園駅]]から徒歩10分程度 *[[北海道中央バス]]月寒中央通4丁目[[バス停留所|停留所]]から徒歩5分程度 == 出典 == {{reflist}} ==外部リンク== * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ 月寒神社] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 月寒神社] - 北海道神社庁 * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきさむしんしや}} [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧村社]] [[Category:豊平区の歴史]] [[Category:豊平区の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Tsukisamu Shrine | Image = [[File:Tsukisamu-jinja main shrine.jpg|250px|Tsukisamu Shrine]]
Main hall | Location = 4-chōme, Nishi 3-jō, Tsukisamu, Toyohira-ku, [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]], Japan | Latitude degrees = 43 | Latitude minutes = 1 | Latitude seconds = 55 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 141 | Longitude minutes = 23 | Longitude seconds = 20.3 | E/W = E | Map code = JP | Map = Sapporo2 | Deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]]
[[Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto]]
Umashimaji-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|title=Tsukisamu Shrine|url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01022.html|publisher=Hokkaido Shrine Association|accessdate=2018-06-06}} | Rank = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Founded = [[Meiji]] 17 (1884) | Festival = September 8 }} '''Tsukisamu Shrine''' (月寒神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku]], [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]]. The shrine shares historical roots with [[Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine]]. Its [[village shrine]] status in the pre-war [[Shinto shrine ranking system]] highlights its regional significance. ## History The shrine was established in [[1884]] (Meiji 17) by settlers from [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], who enshrined the deities of [[Itsukushima Shrine]] along with the Inari deity. Initially located in the [[Fukuzumi]] area, it was formally recognized in [[1900]] (Meiji 33) as "Nishiyama Shrine" and later renamed "Tsukisamu Shrine" in [[1903]] (Meiji 36). Subsequently, the shrine relocated to its current site in [[1918]] (Taisho 7) and was elevated to [[village shrine]] status. Over the years, the shrine has undergone several renovations, including the construction of a new shrine hall, assembly hall, and administrative office in [[1971]] (Showa 46). Today, the shrine serves as a spiritual and cultural center in the Tsukisamu district. ## Deities Enshrined - **[[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]]** (倉稲魂命): A deity of agriculture and rice cultivation. - **[[Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]]** (大山祇命): A mountain god and guardian of nature. - **[[Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto]]** (市杵島姫命): A goddess associated with water, prosperity, and the arts. - **Umashimaji-no-Mikoto** (宇摩志麻遲命): Known for his connection to divine lineage and governance. ## Festivals and Events The main annual festival takes place on **September 8**, though it is commonly observed on the second Sunday of September for local convenience. The shrine's proximity to schools and parks has fostered strong community ties, making it a focal point of local celebrations. ## Accessibility - **By subway**: Approximately 10 minutes on foot from [[Tsukisamu-Chūō Station]] or [[Misono Station]] on the [[Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line]]. - **By bus**: A 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu-Chūōdōri 4-chōme" bus stop served by [[Hokkaido Chūō Bus]]. ## Nearby Landmarks - **[[Tsukisamu Park]]**: A popular urban park adjacent to the shrine. - **Tsukisamu Elementary School**: Located near the shrine grounds, contributing to its role as a community hub. ## References {{reflist}} ## External Links - [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Official website] - [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Hokkaido Shrine Association - Tsukisamu Shrine] - {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukisamu Shrine}} [[Category:Sapporo Shrines]] [[Category:Hokkaido Shrines]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]] [[Category:Religious Sites Established in 1884]]

Umashimaji-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|title=Tsukisamu Shrine|url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01022.html|publisher=Hokkaido Shrine Association|accessdate=2018-06-06}} | Rank = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Founded = [[Meiji]] 17 (1884) | Festival = September 8 }} '''Tsukisamu Shrine''' (月寒神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Toyohira-ku]], [[Sapporo]], [[Hokkaido]]. The shrine shares historical roots with [[Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine]]. Its [[village shrine]] status in the pre-war [[Shinto shrine ranking system]] highlights its regional significance. ## History The shrine was established in [[1884]] (Meiji 17) by settlers from [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], who enshrined the deities of [[Itsukushima Shrine]] along with the Inari deity. Initially located in the [[Fukuzumi]] area, it was formally recognized in [[1900]] (Meiji 33) as "Nishiyama Shrine" and later renamed "Tsukisamu Shrine" in [[1903]] (Meiji 36). Subsequently, the shrine relocated to its current site in [[1918]] (Taisho 7) and was elevated to [[village shrine]] status. Over the years, the shrine has undergone several renovations, including the construction of a new shrine hall, assembly hall, and administrative office in [[1971]] (Showa 46). Today, the shrine serves as a spiritual and cultural center in the Tsukisamu district. ## Deities Enshrined - **[[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]]** (倉稲魂命): A deity of agriculture and rice cultivation. - **[[Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]]** (大山祇命): A mountain god and guardian of nature. - **[[Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto]]** (市杵島姫命): A goddess associated with water, prosperity, and the arts. - **Umashimaji-no-Mikoto** (宇摩志麻遲命): Known for his connection to divine lineage and governance. ## Festivals and Events The main annual festival takes place on **September 8**, though it is commonly observed on the second Sunday of September for local convenience. The shrine's proximity to schools and parks has fostered strong community ties, making it a focal point of local celebrations. ## Accessibility - **By subway**: Approximately 10 minutes on foot from [[Tsukisamu-Chūō Station]] or [[Misono Station]] on the [[Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line]]. - **By bus**: A 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu-Chūōdōri 4-chōme" bus stop served by [[Hokkaido Chūō Bus]]. ## Nearby Landmarks - **[[Tsukisamu Park]]**: A popular urban park adjacent to the shrine. - **Tsukisamu Elementary School**: Located near the shrine grounds, contributing to its role as a community hub. ## References {{reflist}} ## External Links - [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Official website] - [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Hokkaido Shrine Association - Tsukisamu Shrine] - {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukisamu Shrine}} [[Category:Sapporo Shrines]] [[Category:Hokkaido Shrines]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]] [[Category:Religious Sites Established in 1884]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:kk]]

The shrine's proximity to schools and parks has fostered strong community ties, making it a focal point of local celebrations. ## Accessibility - **By subway**: Approximately 10 minutes on foot from [[Tsukisamu-Chūō Station]] or [[Misono Station]] on the [[Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line]]. - **By bus**: A 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu-Chūōdōri 4-chōme" bus stop served by [[Hokkaido Chūō Bus]]. ## Nearby Landmarks - **[[Tsukisamu Park]]**: A popular urban park adjacent to the shrine. - **Tsukisamu Elementary School**: Located near the shrine grounds, contributing to its role as a community hub. ## References {{reflist}} ## External Links - [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Official website] - [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Hokkaido Shrine Association - Tsukisamu Shrine] - {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukisamu Shrine}} [[Category:Sapporo Shrines]] [[Category:Hokkaido Shrines]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]] [[Category:Religious Sites Established in 1884]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:broken nos]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]]

{{Shrine |Name = Tsukisamu Shrine |Image = [[File:Tsukisamu-jinja main shrine.jpg|250px|Tsukisamu Shrine]]
Haiden |Location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Sapporo City|ja|札幌市}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward|ja|豊平区}}, Tsukisamu Nishi 3-jo 4-chome 1-56 | Latitude = 43 | Latitude Minutes = 1 | Latitude Seconds = 55 | N = N | Longitude = 141 | Longitude Minutes = 23 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Sapporo2 |Deities = {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}
{{ill|Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命}}
{{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}}
Umashimaji-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01022.html |title=Tsukisamu Shrine |publisher = Hokkaido Shrine Association Official Website |accessdate =2018-06-06}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Founded = {{ill|Meiji 17|ja|明治17年}} (1884) |Festivals = September 8 |Rituals = }} '''Tsukisamu Shrine''' (月寒神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Sapporo City|ja|札幌市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}}. == History == In 1884 (Meiji 17), settlers from {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} enshrined two deities, including a spirit from {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}}, in the current {{ill|Fukuzumi|ja|福住}} district. On September 7, 1900 (Meiji 33), it was officially established as an unranked shrine under the name "Nishiyama Shrine" by Hokkaido Government Directive 2307. In 1903 (Meiji 36), it was renamed "Tsukisamu Shrine" and received permission to relocate to Tsukisamu Village Lot 17. This marked its separation from Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine. On February 16, 1918 (Taisho 7), permission was granted to move the shrine to Lot 498 in Tsukisamu (its current location), with the move completed by March 4 of the same year. On November 2, 1918, it was elevated to the status of a village shrine and designated a {{ill|Heihaku Supply Shrine|ja|幣帛供進神社}}. The shrine buildings, including the main shrine, hall, and office, were rebuilt on December 2, 1971 (Showa 46). While the official festival date is September 8, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September for local convenience. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園}} and {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|ja|月寒中央駅}} on the {{ill|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line|ja|札幌市営地下鉄東豊線}}. * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Misono Station|ja|美園駅}} on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of {{ill|Hokkaido Chūō Bus|ja|北海道中央バス}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[category:new pages]] [[ja:月寒神社]]

It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}}. == History == In [[1884]] ({{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} 17), settlers from [[Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ({{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正}} 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ({{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和}} 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|ja|月寒中央駅}} on the {{ill|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line|ja|札幌市営地下鉄東豊線}}. * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Misono Station|ja|美園駅}} on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of {{ill|Hokkaido Chūō Bus|ja|北海道中央バス}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ja:月寒神社]]

It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}}. == History == In [[1884]] ({{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} 17), settlers from [[Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ({{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正}} 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ({{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和}} 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|ja|月寒中央駅}} on the {{ill|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line|ja|札幌市営地下鉄東豊線}}. * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Misono Station|ja|美園駅}} on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of {{ill|Hokkaido Chūō Bus|ja|北海道中央バス}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:月寒神社]]

Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ({{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和}} 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|ja|月寒中央駅}} on the {{ill|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line|ja|札幌市営地下鉄東豊線}}. * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Misono Station|ja|美園駅}} on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of {{ill|Hokkaido Chūō Bus|ja|北海道中央バス}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:月寒神社]]

It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}}. == History == In [[1884]] ({{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} 17), settlers from [[Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ({{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正}} 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ({{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和}} 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|ja|月寒中央駅}} on the {{ill|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line|ja|札幌市営地下鉄東豊線}}. * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Misono Station|ja|美園駅}} on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of {{ill|Hokkaido Chūō Bus|ja|北海道中央バス}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:月寒神社]]

It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}}. == History == In [[1884]] ({{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} 17), settlers from [[Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ({{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正}} 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ({{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和}} 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|ja|月寒中央駅}} on the {{ill|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line|ja|札幌市営地下鉄東豊線}}. * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Misono Station|ja|美園駅}} on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of {{ill|Hokkaido Chūō Bus|ja|北海道中央バス}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[ja:月寒神社]]

Umashimaji-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01022.html |title=Tsukisamu Shrine |publisher = Hokkaido Shrine Association Official Website |accessdate =2018-06-06}} |type = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Meiji 17|ja|明治17年}} (1884) |Festivals = September 8 |Rituals = }} '''Tsukisamu Shrine''' (月寒神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Sapporo City|ja|札幌市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代type制度}}. == History == In [[1884]] ({{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} 17), settlers from [[Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ({{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正}} 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ({{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和}} 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|ja|月寒中央駅}} on the {{ill|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line|ja|札幌市営地下鉄東豊線}}. * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Misono Station|ja|美園駅}} on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of {{ill|Hokkaido Chūō Bus|ja|北海道中央バス}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tsukisamu Shrine]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:月寒神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:11]]

Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ({{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和}} 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsukisamu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsukisamu-jinja main shrine.jpg|250px|Tsukisamu Shrine]]
Haiden |location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Sapporo City|ja|札幌市}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward|ja|豊平区}}, Tsukisamu Nishi 3-jo 4-chome 1-56 | Latitude = 43 | Latitude Minutes = 1 | Latitude Seconds = 55 | N = N | Longitude = 141 | Longitude Minutes = 23 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Sapporo2 |Deities = {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}
{{ill|Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命}}
{{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}}
Umashimaji-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01022.html |title=Tsukisamu Shrine |publisher = Hokkaido Shrine Association Official Website |accessdate =2018-06-06}} |type = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Meiji 17|ja|明治17年}} (1884) |Festivals = September 8 |Rituals = }} '''Tsukisamu Shrine''' (月寒神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Sapporo City|ja|札幌市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == In [[1884]] ({{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} 17), settlers from [[Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ({{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正}} 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ({{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和}} 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|ja|月寒中央駅}} on the {{ill|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line|ja|札幌市営地下鉄東豊線}}. * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Misono Station|ja|美園駅}} on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of {{ill|Hokkaido Chūō Bus|ja|北海道中央バス}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tsukisamu Shrine]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:月寒神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

{{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}
Umashimaji-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01022.html |title=Tsukisamu Shrine |publisher = Hokkaido Shrine Association Official Website |accessdate =2018-06-06}} |type = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Meiji 17|ja|明治17年}} (1884) |Festivals = September 8 |Rituals = }} '''Tsukisamu Shrine''' (月寒神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Sapporo City|ja|札幌市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == In [[1884]] ({{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} 17), settlers from [[Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ({{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正}} 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ({{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和}} 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|ja|月寒中央駅}} on the {{ill|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line|ja|札幌市営地下鉄東豊線}}. * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Misono Station|ja|美園駅}} on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of {{ill|Hokkaido Chūō Bus|ja|北海道中央バス}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Tsukisamu Shrine]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:月寒神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:sukisamu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|ja|月寒中央駅}} on the {{ill|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line|ja|札幌市営地下鉄東豊線}}. * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Misono Station|ja|美園駅}} on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of {{ill|Hokkaido Chūō Bus|ja|北海道中央バス}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Tsukisamu Shrine]] [[Category:Sqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:月寒神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:sukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == In [[1884]] ({{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} 17), settlers from [[Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ({{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正}} 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ({{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和}} 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|ja|月寒中央駅}} on the {{ill|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line|ja|札幌市営地下鉄東豊線}}. * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Misono Station|ja|美園駅}} on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of {{ill|Hokkaido Chūō Bus|ja|北海道中央バス}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Tsukisamu Shrine]] [[Category:S11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:月寒神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:sukisamu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]][[Category:11]]

It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == In [[1884]] ({{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} 17), settlers from [[Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住}} district. It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == In [[1884]] ({{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} 17), settlers from [[Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ({{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和}} 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|ja|月寒中央駅}} on the {{ill|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line|ja|札幌市営地下鉄東豊線}}. * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Misono Station|ja|美園駅}} on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of {{ill|Hokkaido Chūō Bus|ja|北海道中央バス}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsukisamu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsukisamu-jinja main shrine.jpg|250px|Tsukisamu Shrine]]
Haiden |location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, {{ill|Sapporo City|ja|札幌市}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward|ja|豊平区}}, Tsukisamu Nishi 3-jo 4-chome 1-56 | Latitude = 43 | Latitude Minutes = 1 | Latitude Seconds = 55 | N = N | Longitude = 141 | Longitude Minutes = 23 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Sapporo2 |Deities = {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}
{{ill|Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
{{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}
Umashimaji-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01022.html |title=Tsukisamu Shrine |publisher = Hokkaido Shrine Association Official Website |accessdate =2018-06-06}} |type = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Meiji 17|ja|明治17th year }} (1884) |Festivals = September 8 |Rituals = }} '''Tsukisamu Shrine''' (月寒神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward|ja|豊平区}}, {{ill|Sapporo City|ja|札幌市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == In [[1884]] ({{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} 17), settlers from [[Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ({{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正}} 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ({{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和}} 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|ja|月寒中央駅}} on the {{ill|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line|ja|札幌市営地下鉄東豊線}}. * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Misono Station|ja|美園駅}} on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of {{ill|Hokkaido Chūō Bus|ja|北海道中央バス}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Tsukisamu Shrine]] [[Category:S11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]] [[ja:月寒神社]] [[sn:sukisamu Shrine]]

The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|12=draft}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=draft}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|12=draft}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|ja|月寒中央駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line|ja|札幌市営地下鉄東豊線|12=draft}}. * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Misono Station|ja|美園駅|12=draft}} on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of {{ill|Hokkaido Chūō Bus|ja|北海道中央バス|12=draft}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Tsukisamu Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]] [[ja:月寒神社]] [[sn:sukisamu Shrine]]

It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|12=draft}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. == History == In [[1884]] ({{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|12=draft}} 17), settlers from [[Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|12=draft}} and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住|12=draft}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|12=draft}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ({{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正|12=draft}} 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|12=draft}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ({{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|12=draft|12=draft}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=draft|12=draft}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|ja|月寒中央駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line|ja|札幌市営地下鉄東豊線|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Misono Station|ja|美園駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of {{ill|Hokkaido Chūō Bus|ja|北海道中央バス|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Tsukisamu Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]] [[ja:月寒神社]] [[sn:sukisamu Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsukisamu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsukisamu-jinja main shrine.jpg|250px|Tsukisamu Shrine]]
Haiden |location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sapporo City|ja|札幌市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward|ja|豊平区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsukisamu Nishi 3-jo 4-chome 1-56 | Latitude = 43 | Latitude Minutes = 1 | Latitude Seconds = 55 | N = N | Longitude = 141 | Longitude Minutes = 23 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Sapporo2 |Deities = {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Umashimaji-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01022.html |title=Tsukisamu Shrine |publisher = Hokkaido Shrine Association Official Website |accessdate =2018-06-06}} |type = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Meiji 17|ja|明治17th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1884) |Festivals = September 8 |Rituals = }} '''Tsukisamu Shrine''' (月寒神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward|ja|豊平区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sapporo City|ja|札幌市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == In [[1884]] ({{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 17), settlers from [[Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住|qq=draft|qq=draft}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ({{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ({{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|ja|月寒中央駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line|ja|札幌市営地下鉄東豊線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Misono Station|ja|美園駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of {{ill|Hokkaido Chūō Bus|ja|北海道中央バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Tsukisamu Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]] [[ja:月寒神社]] [[sn:sukisamu Shrine]]

revision.comment: 34 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:月寒神社 up to rev 98103416
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=月寒神社 |画像=[[ファイル:Tsukisamu-jinja main shrine.jpg|250px|月寒神社]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]]月寒西3条4丁目1-56 | 緯度度 = 43 | 緯度分 = 1 | 緯度秒 = 55 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 141 |経度分 = 23 | 経度秒 = 20.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Sapporo2 |祭神=[[倉稲魂命]]
[[大山祇命]]
[[市杵島姫命]]
宇摩志麻遲命{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01022.html |title=月寒神社　 |publisher =北海道神社庁 公式ホームページ|accessdate =2018-06-06}} |社格=旧[[村社]] |創建=[[明治]]17年（[[1884年]]） |例祭=9月8日 |神事= }} '''月寒神社'''（つきさむじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[豊平区]][[月寒|月寒西]]にある[[神社]]。[[福住厳島神社]]とは同根である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 由緒 == [[1884年]]（[[明治]]17年）に、[[広島県]]出身の移住者が[[厳島神社]]の御分霊と稲荷神の2柱を現在の[[福住 (札幌市)|福住]]地区に祀った。[[1900年]]（明治33年）[[9月7日]]には[[北海道]]庁指令2307号を以って、無格社「'''西山神社'''」と公称し、創立許可を得た。[[1903年]]（明治36年）[[10月28日]]に「'''月寒神社'''」と改称し、月寒村17番地への移転を出願し、翌[[1904年]]（明治37年）[[5月24日]]には移転許可を受ける。これにより、現在の[[福住厳島神社]]と分離することになる。 [[1918年]]（[[大正]]7年）[[2月16日]]には[[月寒]]498番地（現在地）への移転許可を出願し、同年[[3月4日]]に移転許可を受ける。同年[[11月2日]]には村社に昇格し、幣帛供進神社に指定される。[[1971年]]（[[昭和]]46年）[[12月2日]]には社殿・参集所・社務所御造営が竣功した。 なお、本来の例大祭は9月8日であるが、隣接して[[札幌市立月寒小学校|小学校]]が所在することなどから町内会等との申し合わせもあり、現在は毎年9月の第2日曜日に執り行われている。また[[都市公園]]である[[月寒公園]]とも隣接する。 == 祭神 == *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（うがのみたまのみこと） *[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]]（おおやまつみのみこと） *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（いちきしまひめのみこと） *[[ウマシマジ|宇摩志麻遲命]]（うましまぢのみこと） == 交通手段 == *[[札幌市営地下鉄東豊線]][[月寒中央駅]]から徒歩10分程度 *札幌市営地下鉄東豊線[[美園駅]]から徒歩10分程度 *[[北海道中央バス]]月寒中央通4丁目[[バス停留所|停留所]]から徒歩5分程度 == 出典 == {{reflist}} ==外部リンク== * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ 月寒神社] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 月寒神社] - 北海道神社庁 * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきさむしんしや}} [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧村社]] [[Category:豊平区の歴史]] [[Category:豊平区の建築物]]

The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|ja|月寒中央駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line|ja|札幌市営地下鉄東豊線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Misono Station|ja|美園駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of {{ill|Hokkaido Chūō Bus|ja|北海道中央バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Tsukisamu Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]] [[ja:月寒神社]] [[sn:sukisamu Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|月寒神社|version=98103416|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsukisamu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsukisamu-jinja main shrine.jpg|250px|Tsukisamu Shrine]]
Haiden |location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sapporo City|ja|札幌市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward|ja|豊平区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsukisamu Nishi 3-jo 4-chome 1-56 | Latitude = 43 | Latitude Minutes = 1 | Latitude Seconds = 55 | N = N | Longitude = 141 | Longitude Minutes = 23 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Sapporo2 |Deities = {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Umashimaji-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01022.html |title=Tsukisamu Shrine |publisher = Hokkaido Shrine Association Official Website |accessdate =2018-06-06}} |type = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Meiji 17|ja|明治17th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1884) |Festivals = September 8 |Rituals = }} '''Tsukisamu Shrine''' (月寒神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyohira Ward|ja|豊平区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sapporo City|ja|札幌市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == In [[1884]] ({{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 17), settlers from [[Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住|qq=draft|qq=draft}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ({{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ({{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|ja|月寒中央駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line|ja|札幌市営地下鉄東豊線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Misono Station|ja|美園駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of {{ill|Hokkaido Chūō Bus|ja|北海道中央バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Tsukisamu Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]] [[ja:月寒神社]] [[sn:sukisamu Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|月寒神社|version=98103416|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == In [[1884]] ({{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 17), settlers from [[Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住|qq=draft|qq=draft}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ({{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ({{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|ja|月寒中央駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line|ja|札幌市営地下鉄東豊線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Misono Station|ja|美園駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of {{ill|Hokkaido Chūō Bus|ja|北海道中央バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|月寒神社|version=98103416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:Tsukisamu Shrine]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Tsukisamu Shrine]] [[ja:月寒神社]] [[sn:sukisamu Shrine]]
