The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Park|qq=|1=Tsukisamu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516037}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhō Line|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line]]. * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Misono Station|Misono Station]] on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of [[:en:Hokkaido Chuo Bus|Hokkaido Chūō Bus]]. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|qq=|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == In [[:en:1884|1884]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 17), settlers from [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|qq=|1=Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|qq=|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō period]] 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu|qq=|1=Tsukisamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516033}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa period]] 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Elementary School|qq=|1=Tsukisamu Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521368}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Park|qq=|1=Tsukisamu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516037}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhō Line|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line]]. * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Misono Station|Misono Station]] on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of [[:en:Hokkaido Chuo Bus|Hokkaido Chūō Bus]]. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|月寒神社|version=98103416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:月寒神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] {{Commons category|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11516040}}

Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa period]] 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Elementary School|1=Tsukisamu Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521368}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Park|1=Tsukisamu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516037}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhō Line|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line]]. * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Misono Station|Misono Station]] on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of [[:en:Hokkaido Chuo Bus|Hokkaido Chūō Bus]]. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Umashimaji-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01022.html |title=Tsukisamu Shrine |publisher = Hokkaido Shrine Association Official Website |accessdate =2018-06-06}} |type = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Meiji 17|ja|明治17年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 17|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1884年|1=1884|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7819}} (1884) |Festivals = September 8 |Rituals = | location = {{ill|Toyohira-ku|WD=Q1134225}} | coordinates = {{Coord|43.031944|N|141.388889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime|WD=Q11059454}}; {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728}}; {{ill|Umashimaji|WD=Q10946169}}; {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} }} '''Tsukisamu Shrine''' (月寒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu|1=Tsukisamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516033}}, [[:en:Toyohira-ku, Sapporo|Toyohira Ward]], [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == In [[:en:1884|1884]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 17), settlers from [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|1=Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō period]] 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu|1=Tsukisamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516033}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa period]] 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Elementary School|1=Tsukisamu Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521368}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Park|1=Tsukisamu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516037}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhō Line|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line]]. * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Misono Station|Misono Station]] on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of [[:en:Hokkaido Chuo Bus|Hokkaido Chūō Bus]]. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - [[sn:commons:Category:Tsukisamu-jinja]][[ja:月寒神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11516040]] Wikidata name: Tsukisamu Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsukisamu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsukisamu-jinja main shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo City]], [[:en:Toyohira-ku, Sapporo|Toyohira Ward]], Tsukisamu Nishi 3-jo 4-chome 1-56 | Latitude = 43 | Latitude Minutes = 1 | Latitude Seconds = 55 | N = N | Longitude = 141 | Longitude Minutes = 23 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Sapporo2 |Deities = {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}
{{ill|Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
Umashimaji-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01022.html |title=Tsukisamu Shrine |publisher = Hokkaido Shrine Association Official Website |accessdate =2018-06-06}} |type = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Meiji 17|ja|明治17年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 17|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1884年|1=1884|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7819}} (1884) |Festivals = September 8 |Rituals = | location = {{ill|Toyohira-ku|WD=Q1134225}} | coordinates = {{Coord|43.031944|N|141.388889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime|WD=Q11059454}}; {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728}}; {{ill|Umashimaji|WD=Q10946169}}; {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} }} '''Tsukisamu Shrine''' (月寒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu|1=Tsukisamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516033}}, [[:en:Toyohira-ku, Sapporo|Toyohira Ward]], [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == In [[:en:1884|1884]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 17), settlers from [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|1=Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō period]] 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu|1=Tsukisamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516033}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa period]] 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Elementary School|1=Tsukisamu Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521368}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Park|1=Tsukisamu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516037}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhō Line|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line]]. * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Misono Station|Misono Station]] on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of [[:en:Hokkaido Chuo Bus|Hokkaido Chūō Bus]]. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|月寒神社|version=98103416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:月寒神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] {{Commons category|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11516040}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō period]] 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu|1=Tsukisamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516033}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa period]] 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Elementary School|1=Tsukisamu Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521368}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Park|1=Tsukisamu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516037}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhō Line|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line]]. * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Misono Station|Misono Station]] on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of [[:en:Hokkaido Chuo Bus|Hokkaido Chūō Bus]]. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - [[sn:commons:Category:Tsukisamu-jinja]][[ja:月寒神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11516040]] Wikidata name: Tsukisamu Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsukisamu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsukisamu-jinja main shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo City]], [[:en:Toyohira-ku, Sapporo|Toyohira Ward]], Tsukisamu Nishi 3-jo 4-chome 1-56 | Latitude = 43 | Latitude Minutes = 1 | Latitude Seconds = 55 | N = N | Longitude = 141 | Longitude Minutes = 23 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Sapporo2 |Deities = {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}
{{ill|Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
Umashimaji-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01022.html |title=Tsukisamu Shrine |publisher = Hokkaido Shrine Association Official Website |accessdate =2018-06-06}} |type = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Meiji 17|ja|明治17年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 17|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1884年|1=1884|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7819}} (1884) |Festivals = September 8 |Rituals = | location = {{ill|Toyohira-ku|WD=Q1134225}} | coordinates = {{Coord|43.031944|N|141.388889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime|WD=Q11059454}}; {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728}}; {{ill|Umashimaji|WD=Q10946169}}; {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} }} '''Tsukisamu Shrine''' (月寒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu|1=Tsukisamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516033}}, [[:en:Toyohira-ku, Sapporo|Toyohira Ward]], [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == In [[:en:1884|1884]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 17), settlers from [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|1=Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō period]] 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu|1=Tsukisamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516033}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa period]] 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Elementary School|1=Tsukisamu Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521368}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Park|1=Tsukisamu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516037}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhō Line|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line]]. * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Misono Station|Misono Station]] on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of [[:en:Hokkaido Chuo Bus|Hokkaido Chūō Bus]]. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|月寒神社|version=98103416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:月寒神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] {{Commons category|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11516040}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q11516040}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Umashimaji-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01022.html |title=Tsukisamu Shrine |publisher = Hokkaido Shrine Association Official Website |accessdate =2018-06-06}} |type = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Meiji 17|ja|明治17年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 17|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1884年|1=1884|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7819}} (1884) |Festivals = September 8 |Rituals = | location = {{ill|Toyohira-ku|WD=Q1134225}} | coordinates = {{Coord|43.031944|N|141.388889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime|WD=Q11059454}}; {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728}}; {{ill|Umashimaji|WD=Q10946169}}; {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} }} '''Tsukisamu Shrine''' (月寒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu|1=Tsukisamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516033}}, [[:en:Toyohira-ku, Sapporo|Toyohira Ward]], [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == In [[:en:1884|1884]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 17), settlers from [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|1=Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō period]] 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu|1=Tsukisamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516033}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa period]] 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Elementary School|1=Tsukisamu Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521368}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Park|1=Tsukisamu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516037}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhō Line|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line]]. * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Misono Station|Misono Station]] on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of [[:en:Hokkaido Chuo Bus|Hokkaido Chūō Bus]]. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|月寒神社|version=98103416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:月寒神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] {{Commons category|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11516040}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11516040}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō period]] 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu|1=Tsukisamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516033}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa period]] 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Elementary School|1=Tsukisamu Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521368}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Park|1=Tsukisamu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516037}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhō Line|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line]]. * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Misono Station|Misono Station]] on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of [[:en:Hokkaido Chuo Bus|Hokkaido Chūō Bus]]. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - {{ill|Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}

Umashimaji-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01022.html |title=Tsukisamu Shrine |publisher = Hokkaido Shrine Association Official Website |accessdate =2018-06-06}} |type = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Meiji 17|ja|明治17年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 17|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1884年|1=1884|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7819}} (1884) |Festivals = September 8 |Rituals = | location = {{ill|Toyohira-ku|WD=Q1134225}} | coordinates = {{Coord|43.031944|N|141.388889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime|WD=Q11059454}}; {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728}}; {{ill|Umashimaji|WD=Q10946169}}; {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} }} '''Tsukisamu Shrine''' (月寒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu|1=Tsukisamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516033}}, [[:en:Toyohira-ku, Sapporo|Toyohira Ward]], [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == In [[:en:1884|1884]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 17), settlers from [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|1=Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō period]] 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu|1=Tsukisamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516033}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa period]] 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Elementary School|1=Tsukisamu Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521368}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Park|1=Tsukisamu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516037}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhō Line|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line]]. * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Misono Station|Misono Station]] on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of [[:en:Hokkaido Chuo Bus|Hokkaido Chūō Bus]]. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Park|1=Tsukisamu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516037}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhō Line|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line]]. * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Misono Station|Misono Station]] on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of [[:en:Hokkaido Chuo Bus|Hokkaido Chūō Bus]]. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - [[commons:Category:Tsukisamu-jinja]][[ja:月寒神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsukisamu-jinja]][[ja:月寒神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11516040]] Wikidata name: Tsukisamu Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsukisamu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsukisamu-jinja main shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo City]], [[:en:Toyohira-ku, Sapporo|Toyohira Ward]], Tsukisamu Nishi 3-jo 4-chome 1-56 | Latitude = 43 | Latitude Minutes = 1 | Latitude Seconds = 55 | N = N | Longitude = 141 | Longitude Minutes = 23 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Sapporo2 |Deities = {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}
{{ill|Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
Umashimaji-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01022.html |title=Tsukisamu Shrine |publisher = Hokkaido Shrine Association Official Website |accessdate =2018-06-06}} |type = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Meiji 17|ja|明治17年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 17|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1884年|1=1884|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7819}} (1884) |Festivals = September 8 |Rituals = | location = {{ill|Toyohira-ku|WD=Q1134225}} | coordinates = {{Coord|43.031944|N|141.388889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime|WD=Q11059454}}; {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728}}; {{ill|Umashimaji|WD=Q10946169}}; {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} }} '''Tsukisamu Shrine''' (月寒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu|1=Tsukisamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516033}}, [[:en:Toyohira-ku, Sapporo|Toyohira Ward]], [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == In [[:en:1884|1884]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 17), settlers from [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|1=Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō period]] 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu|1=Tsukisamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516033}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa period]] 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Elementary School|1=Tsukisamu Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521368}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Park|1=Tsukisamu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516037}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhō Line|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line]]. * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Misono Station|Misono Station]] on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of [[:en:Hokkaido Chuo Bus|Hokkaido Chūō Bus]]. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|月寒神社|version=98103416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:月寒神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] {{Commons category|Tsukisamu-jinja}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11516040}}

Umashimaji-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01022.html |title=Tsukisamu Shrine |publisher = Hokkaido Shrine Association Official Website |accessdate =2018-06-06}} |type = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Meiji 17|ja|明治17年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 17|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1884年|1=1884|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7819}} (1884) |Festivals = September 8 |Rituals = | location = {{ill|Toyohira-ku|WD=Q1134225}} | coordinates = {{Coord|43.031944|N|141.388889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime|WD=Q11059454}}; {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728}}; {{ill|Umashimaji|WD=Q10946169}}; {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} }} '''Tsukisamu Shrine''' (月寒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu|1=Tsukisamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516033}}, [[:en:Toyohira-ku, Sapporo|Toyohira Ward]], [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == In [[:en:1884|1884]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 17), settlers from [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|1=Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō period]] 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu|1=Tsukisamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516033}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa period]] 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Elementary School|1=Tsukisamu Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521368}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Park|1=Tsukisamu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516037}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhō Line|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line]]. * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Misono Station|Misono Station]] on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of [[:en:Hokkaido Chuo Bus|Hokkaido Chūō Bus]]. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|月寒神社|version=98103416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:月寒神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] {{Commons category|Tsukisamu-jinja}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11516040}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Elementary School|1=Tsukisamu Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521368}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Park|1=Tsukisamu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516037}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhō Line|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line]]. * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Misono Station|Misono Station]] on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of [[:en:Hokkaido Chuo Bus|Hokkaido Chūō Bus]]. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|月寒神社|version=98103416|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:月寒神社]] {{Commons category|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11516040}} [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
Umashimaji-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01022.html |title=Tsukisamu Shrine |publisher = Hokkaido Shrine Association Official Website |accessdate =2018-06-06}} |type = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = {{ill|Meiji 17|ja|明治17年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 17|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1884年|1=1884|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7819}} (1884) |Festivals = September 8 |Rituals = | location = {{ill|Toyohira-ku|WD=Q1134225}} | coordinates = {{Coord|43.031944|N|141.388889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime|WD=Q11059454}}; {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728}}; {{ill|Umashimaji|WD=Q10946169}}; {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} }} '''Tsukisamu Shrine''' (月寒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu|1=Tsukisamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516033}}, [[:en:Toyohira-ku, Sapporo|Toyohira Ward]], [[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo City]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. It shares roots with {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == In [[:en:1884|1884]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 17), settlers from [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県]] enshrined two deities—one spirit from [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] and an Inari deity—in what is now the {{ill|Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|ja|福住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|1=Fukuzumi (Sapporo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591777}} district. On [[September 7, 1900]] (Meiji 33), by Hokkaidō Government Directive No. 2307, the shrine was officially named "'''Nishiyama Shrine'''" (西山神社) and received approval for its establishment. On [[October 28, 1903]] (Meiji 36), the shrine was renamed "'''Tsukisamu Shrine'''" (月寒神社) and applied for relocation to lot 17 of Tsukisamu Village. The relocation was approved on [[May 24, 1904]] (Meiji 37), separating it from the present-day {{ill|Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|ja|福住厳島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|1=Fukuzumi Itsukushima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591788}}. On [[February 16, 1918]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō period]] 7), the shrine applied to move to lot 498 of {{ill|Tsukisamu|ja|月寒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu|1=Tsukisamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516033}} (its current location), receiving approval on [[March 4, 1918]]. Later that year, on [[November 2, 1918]], it was elevated to the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and designated as a government-supported shrine for ritual offerings (幣帛供進神社). On [[December 2, 1971]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa period]] 46), construction of the shrine building, assembly hall, and shrine office was completed. The original annual festival date was September 8, but due to the proximity of the {{ill|Tsukisamu Elementary School|ja|札幌市立月寒小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Elementary School|1=Tsukisamu Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521368}} and agreements with local neighborhood associations, the festival is now held on the second Sunday of September each year. The shrine is also adjacent to the {{ill|Tsukisamu Park|ja|月寒公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisamu Park|1=Tsukisamu Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516037}}, a municipal park. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (Spirit of Grains) * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Water Goddess) * {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ancestral God) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Tsukisamu-Chūō Station|Tsukisamu-Chūō Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhō Line|Sapporo Municipal Subway Tōhō Line]]. * About a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Misono Station|Misono Station]] on the Tōhō Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Tsukisamu Chūō-dōri 4-chome" bus stop of [[:en:Hokkaido Chuo Bus|Hokkaido Chūō Bus]]. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://tsukisamujinja.com/ Tsukisamu Shrine Official Website] * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%E6%9C%88%E5%AF%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Tsukisamu Shrine - Hokkaido Shrine Association] * {{Instagram|tsukisamu_jinja}} {{commonscat|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|月寒神社|version=98103416|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:月寒神社]] {{Commons category|Tsukisamu-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11516040}} [[Category:Buildings in Toyohira-ku, Sapporo]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Toyohira Ward]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
